mnemonics: SHARED: System-wide, High-performance, Accessible, Reusable, Efficient, Dynamic
keyPoints: Shared libraries are object files that multiple programs can use simultaneously, reducing memory and disk space usage.
They are linked at runtime (dynamic linking) rather than compile time (static linking).
The `ldd` command shows which shared libraries a program depends on, and `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` is an environment variable used to specify their locations.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to set the `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` environment variable, causing a 'library not found' error at runtime.
Creating an application that depends on a specific version of a shared library that may not be present on the target system.
